Sql: WITH base AS NOT MATERIALIZED (SELECT * FROM (VALUES (1, 10), (1, 20), (2, 30)) AS t(k, v)), summ AS NOT MATERIALIZED (SELECT k, COALESCE(SUM(v), 0) AS s FROM base GROUP BY ALL), mat AS MATERIALIZED (SELECT * FROM summ) SELECT k, s, s1, diff FROM (SELECT *, s - s1 AS diff FROM (SELECT L.*, R.k AS k1, R.s AS s1 FROM mat AS L INNER JOIN (SELECT * FROM mat WHERE s > 15) AS R ON L.k = R.k)) ORDER BY k ASC

Kql: datatable(s:string, tag:string)[ "alpha","x", "Beta","x", "café","y", "","z", "O'Brien","x" ] | extend len = strlen(s) | summarize tot = sum(len), names = make_list(s) by tag | where tot > 0 | mv-expand n = names to typeof(string) | summarize cnt = count() by tag | sort by tag asc
Sql: SELECT * FROM (SELECT tag, COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM (SELECT t.*, u.value AS n FROM (SELECT * FROM (SELECT tag, COALESCE(SUM(len), 0) AS tot, COALESCE(LIST(s), []) AS names FROM (SELECT *, LENGTH(CAST(s AS VARCHAR)) AS len FROM (VALUES ('alpha', 'x'), ('Beta', 'x'), ('café', 'y'), ('', 'z'), ('O''Brien', 'x')) AS t(s, tag)) GROUP BY ALL) WHERE tot > 0) AS t CROSS JOIN UNNEST(names) AS u(value)) GROUP BY ALL) ORDER BY tag ASC

Kql: let r0 = range i from 1 to 5 step 1; let r1 = r0 | extend j = i; let r2 = r1 | join kind=inner (r0 | project i2 = i) on $left.i == $right.i2; let r3 = r2 | extend k = i + i2; r3 | project i, i2, k | sort by i asc
Sql: WITH r0 AS NOT MATERIALIZED (SELECT generate_series AS i FROM generate_series(CAST(1 AS BIGINT), CAST(5 AS BIGINT), CAST(1 AS BIGINT))), r1 AS NOT MATERIALIZED (SELECT *, i AS j FROM r0), r2 AS NOT MATERIALIZED (SELECT L.*, R.* RENAME (i AS i1) FROM r1 AS L INNER JOIN (SELECT i AS i2 FROM r0) AS R ON L.i = R.i2), r3 AS NOT MATERIALIZED (SELECT *, i + i2 AS k FROM r2) SELECT i, i2, k FROM r3 ORDER BY i ASC

Kql: datatable(k:long, v:long)[ 1,10, 1,20, 2,30, 2,30 ] | summarize dcount_v = count() by k | order by dcount_v desc, k asc | extend running = row_cumsum(dcount_v) | project k, dcount_v, running

Kql: print a = 1 | union (print a = 2) | union (print a = 3) | summarize s = sum(a), c = count() | extend mean = s / c
Sql: SELECT *, s / c AS mean FROM (SELECT COALESCE(SUM(a), 0) AS s, COUNT(*) AS c FROM (((SELECT 1 AS a) UNION ALL BY NAME (SELECT 2 AS a)) UNION ALL BY NAME (SELECT 3 AS a)))
